---
title: "Logistic Regression"
---

::: {.callout-tip}
## This chapter is a stub — contributions welcome
Fill it in following the two full exemplars
([Summary Statistics](summary-statistics.qmd),
[ANCOVA](ancova.qmd)) and the
[topic template](https://github.com/OWNER/submission-transparency/blob/main/templates/topic-template.qmd).
See the checklist in [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](../CONTRIBUTING.md).
:::

## What a reviewer wants

For a binary endpoint (e.g. responder yes/no), the adjusted **odds ratio** (or a
risk difference from a marginal standardisation) with its confidence interval.

## Suggested minimal approach

- **Fit:** base R `glm(resp ~ TRT + covariate, family = binomial)`.
- **Report:** `exp(coef)` and `exp(confint())` for the odds ratio, or
  `emmeans(fit, ~TRT, type = "response")` for model-predicted probabilities.

## Simulation to build

- Simulate binary outcomes from a logistic model with a **known** treatment
  log-odds, at a large sample size (n ≈ 1000). Confirm the estimated
  log-odds/odds ratio is unbiased and that the Wald/profile CI covers at ~95%.
